message:
refactor(channel): extract poll_recv methods to avoid transient future allocation in stream adapters

Move core receive polling logic from Recv/BroadcastRecv futures into
poll_recv/poll_recv_with_waiter methods on Receiver types directly. This
lets stream adapters (ReceiverStream, BroadcastStream) and HTTP body
types poll receives without creating-and-dropping pinned futures each
poll cycle, reducing allocation overhead in hot paths.

Fix mpsc Receiver::Drop to clear stale recv_waker registration so
dropped receivers don't retain executor state.

Correct mut/immut bindings on oneshot receivers in fault injection tests
per Rust 2024 move semantics — recv() requires &mut self, but try_recv,
is_ready, and is_closed only need &self.

Add waker-registration tests for broadcast, mpsc, and HTTP body streams
to validate the new poll_recv paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
